entitySuffixRule: Must contain the words "limited liability company" or "limited company," or the abbreviations "L.L.C.," "LLC," "L.C.," or "LC." The word "Limited" may be abbreviated "Ltd." and "Company" may be abbreviated "Co."
distinguishabilityNote: The name must not be the same as or deceptively similar to, on the records of the Secretary of State, the name of any person that is not an individual and is incorporated, organized, or authorized to transact business in Nebraska, or any name reserved under § 21-109 or registered under other state laws, including fictitious, assumed, and trade name statutes. A company may apply to the Secretary of State for authorization to use a name that is deceptively similar to an existing one.
nameReservationCostBasis: Nebraska charges $30 to reserve a name filed in writing but $25 filed electronically; the electronic path is the cheaper and more common one.
notes: Nebraska's Uniform Limited Liability Company Act (Neb. Rev. Stat. ch. 21) uses a stricter "same as or deceptively similar" name standard rather than the usual "distinguishable" test, and it folds reserved, fictitious, assumed, and trade names into the screen (§ 21-108). Section 21-108 carries no banned-word list; words such as "bank," "insurance," and "trust" are governed by separate licensing statutes. A reservation lasts 120 days. Nebraska keeps a distinctive formation step: § 21-193 requires a new LLC to publish a Notice of Organization for three successive weeks in a legal newspaper near its designated office and then file proof with the Secretary of State. The LLC formation fee is $100 and a biennial report ($25) is due in odd-numbered years.
